## Lea el siguiente descargo de responsabilidad antes de continuar
Por favor tenga en cuenta que al continuar usted está ingresando a un sitio web mantenido directa o indirectamente por un tercero (el “**Sitio Externo** ”), y que lo hace bajo su propio riesgo. El Sitio Externo es un sitio web de terceros “enlazado” según lo referido en el aviso de _Exclusión de Responsabilidad_ (el “**Aviso** ”) disponible en [www.first-quantum.com](http://www.first-quantum.com), y dicho Aviso es aplicable a su uso del Sitio Externo. Los términos de este Aviso complementan el Aviso original.
First Quantum Minerals Ltd., incluidas sus filiales y subsidiarias (“**First Quantum** ”), no tiene control alguno sobre el Sitio Externo, sobre los datos u otros contenidos incluidos en él ni sobre sitios web adicionales enlazados. El enlace al Sitio Externo se proporciona únicamente para fines de conveniencia.
Al hacer clic en “Acepto”, usted reconoce y acepta que First Quantum no es responsable ni acepta ninguna responsabilidad por la política de protección de datos, el contenido, los datos ni el funcionamiento técnico del Sitio Enlazado. Asimismo, al ingresar al Sitio Externo, usted también reconoce y acepta que renuncia de manera total e irrevocable a cualquier derecho o reclamación contra First Quantum, y reconoce y acepta además que, en ningún caso, First Quantum, sus directivos, empleados, directores o agentes serán responsables por: (i) daños indirectos, consecuenciales, incidentales, especiales, compensatorios o punitivos; (ii) daños por pérdida de ingresos, pérdida de beneficios comerciales, interrupción del negocio, pérdida de datos o de información empresarial, pérdida o daño a la propiedad; (iii) reclamaciones de terceros; o (iv) cualquier otra pérdida económica que surja de o esté relacionada con el Aviso, este descargo o el Sitio Externo. Al ingresar al Sitio Externo, usted también reconoce y acepta que en ningún caso la responsabilidad acumulada de First Quantum y sus proveedores de servicios, derivada de o relacionada con este descargo o el Aviso, excederá CAD$10.00. En la medida en que algunas jurisdicciones no permitan exclusiones o limitaciones sobre ciertas categorías de daños, es posible que estas exclusiones o limitaciones no le sean aplicables. Al ingresar al Sitio Externo, usted además reconoce y acepta que la exención de garantías y limitación de responsabilidad establecidas en este descargo y en el Aviso aplicarán sin importar las causas, circunstancias o forma de acción que den origen a la pérdida, daño, reclamación o responsabilidad, aun cuando dicha pérdida, daño, reclamación o responsabilidad se base en incumplimiento de contrato (incluyendo, sin limitación, incumplimiento esencial), responsabilidad civil (incluyendo, sin limitación, negligencia), responsabilidad objetiva o cualquier otra teoría legal o equitativa, e incluso si First Quantum y sus proveedores de servicios han sido advertidos de la posibilidad de dicha pérdida, daño, reclamación o responsabilidad. La renuncia y liberación incluye específicamente, sin limitación, cualquier y todo derecho o reclamación relacionados con el tratamiento de datos personales, incluyendo, pero no limitado, a los derechos previstos en cualquier legislación aplicable de protección de datos.
Si en alguna jurisdicción cualquier parte de este descargo fuera considerada inaplicable por un tribunal competente, dicha parte se limitará o eliminará en la medida mínima necesaria y el resto de este descargo continuará en pleno vigor y efecto.
Por favor tenga en cuenta que la información presentada se considera representativa en el momento de su publicación original. Podrían producirse cambios en la información histórica debido a ajustes en normas y procedimientos contables y de reporte.
[Acepto](https://www.sedarplus.ca/landingpage/)
## Uso de medidas y ratios financieros no GAAP
Nuestros resultados financieros se preparan de conformidad con las Normas Internacionales de Información Financiera (NIIF o IFRS, por sus siglas en inglés) emitidas por el _International Accounting Standards Board_. Este sitio web y el Sitio Externo hacen referencia a una serie de medidas y razones financieras que no son medidas reconocidas bajo NIIF y que no tienen un significado estandarizado según las NIIF ni los Principios de Contabilidad Generalmente Aceptados (GAAP) en los Estados Unidos. La información divulgada en el Sitio Externo puede incluir medidas que no son NIIF, las cuales no tienen significados estandarizados según las NIIF, pueden diferir de las utilizadas por otros emisores y no son necesariamente comparables con medidas similares presentadas por otros emisores.
Estas medidas y razones se han derivado de nuestros estados financieros y se han aplicado de manera consistente según corresponda; se utilizan internamente por la administración para medir el desempeño de las operaciones de la Compañía y tienen el propósito de proporcionar información adicional que no debe considerarse de forma aislada frente a las medidas y razones preparadas bajo GAAP. First Quantum divulga estas medidas con el fin de ayudar a comprender los resultados de sus operaciones y de proporcionar información adicional a los inversionistas.
Estas medidas y razones no deben considerarse de manera aislada ni interpretarse como un sustituto, ni superiores, a otras medidas de desempeño preparadas de acuerdo con las NIIF. Una descripción más detallada de las medidas y razones que no son NIIF, incluidas sus conciliaciones, se proporciona en el MD&A que acompaña a los estados financieros de la Compañía, presentados periódicamente en SEDAR+ en [www.sedarplus.com](http://www.sedarplus.com).
[Visitar sitio web](https://vbench.virtuaresearch.com/IR/IAC/?ticker=FM&exchange=TSX)

What we do
First Quantum is a global copper company. We operate long life mines in five continents, employing approximately 14,500 people.

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

About
Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make a user's experience more efficient.  
  
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission.  
  
This site uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third party services that appear on our pages.  
  
You can at any time change or withdraw your consent from the Cookie Declaration on our website.  
  
Learn more about who we are, how you can contact us and how we process personal data in our Privacy Policy.  
  
Please state your consent ID and date when you contact us regarding your consent.
**Do not sell or share my personal information**

# About us
Our unique approach to mining gives us a holistic view, creates leaders at every level, ensures we behave responsibly and has delivered a proven track record.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)About us
## We take a different view
With operating mines spanning five continents, we know that what works in one might not be a perfect match for another. We encourage our people to forge their own path, finding the best way to solve challenges creatively rather than following the beaten track.
Our people are our greatest asset, a team of experts driven by a shared passion to uncover new solutions. We are enabled by an agile approach to work that encourages innovation and helps our projects, and our people, to reach their full potential.
396kt
copper produced in 2025
US$2.4bn
spent in local procurement from nationally registered suppliers
+95%
of employees are nationals in the countries in which we operate
+1.5M
hectares of forest protection programs supported
## At a glance 
We build and operate complex mines to provide the building blocks of society, both for today and in the future. Our teams and our ways of working are designed to generate the maximum value for all stakeholders from our investors to the communities in which we operate.
[Find out more about First Quantum](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/at-a-glance/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-974-1.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1044-2-7-1-2.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1067-2.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1069-2.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1127.jpg)
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4View slide 5
PreviousNext
## Our purpose and values
We work responsibly, both sustainably and ethically, as we meet society’s needs. We look after the environment, we care about the people we work with and those in the communities where we work. Sustainable operations are central to who we are, driven by innovation and our robust governance practices to ensure that we do the right thing.
[Find out what we value](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/our-purpose-and-values/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-970-2-1rs.jpg)
## Leading by example 
First Quantum has a highly skilled leadership team, supported by an experienced Board of Directors. They set the example for our colleagues to follow.
[Meet our leadership team](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1120rs.jpg)
## Transparent practices 
First Quantum is governed with accountability, transparency and integrity. We know that without effective governance, it is impossible to do business the right way.
[Find out more about our governance](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1125rs.jpg)
## Builders and operators
We build and operate our projects, providing a more holistic view of each mine we operate, and providing more opportunities for us to develop and improve on our designs.
[Find out what we do ](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-955-2rs.jpg)
## Sustainability
We care about the people and the places where we work. Our sustainability projects span multiple areas from helping improve education and livelihoods in our communities to protecting animal and plant life in the biodiverse countries where we work.
[Find out about our sustainability](https://www.first-quantum.com/sustainability/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1044-2-3-2-5rs.jpg)
## Insights & stories
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/responsible-mining-banner.jpg)

[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/insights/why-mining-matters/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Coffee-under-the-canopy.jpg)

## Become   
a trusted   
supplier
**Local suppliers**  
We actively search for and work with local suppliers at each of our operations and projects. We want to hear from experts who share our values and are able to help us achieve our goals. Your journey to become a trusted supplier could start today.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/suppliers/)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

# At a glance
One of the world’s top 10 copper producers, we operate globally and employ locally.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)At a glance
## Expert responsible   
miners
First Quantum is a global copper company. We operate long life mines in five continents and employ approximately 14,500 people worldwide.
Known for our specialist technical, engineering, construction and operational skills, we develop and run complex mines and minerals processing plants.
We share the value we generate with our investors, our employees and the people and places surrounding our operations.
396kt
copper produced in 2025
US$2.4bn
spent in local procurement from nationally registered suppliers  

US$28m
invested in collaboration with local communities in 2024
US$9.6bn
cumulative direct contribution to government revenue since (2018-2025)
+95%
local employment
+1.5M
hectares of national protected areas supported
## A unique approach and   
proven track record
While our primary focus is copper, we also produce nickel. As part of our processing operations we realise significant value from a range of other metals including gold, zinc and cobalt.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Copper.jpg)
## [Copper](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/copper/)
Copper is the primary focus of our business and can be found in every site where we operate.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/copper/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Nickel.jpg)
## [Nickel](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/nickel/)
Key to advancing the energy transition.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/nickel/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Other_metals.jpg)
## [Other metals](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/other-metals/)
We realize significant value from a range of metals beyond copper and nickel.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/other-metals/)
## Forging our own   
path since 1996
From our initial reprocessing tailings facilities in Zambia, to the giant Cobre Panamá operation, we’ve used our skills to discover, develop and run complex operations around the world. Here’s our journey so far:
### 2025
#### Kansanshi
The Kansanshi S3 Expansion project reached the final stages of commissioning during the second quarter, ahead of schedule, was officially inaugurated by Zambian President, Hakainde Hichilema in August 2025.
Once completed, it will transition the Kansanshi Mine from a more selective high-grade medium-scale operation to a medium-grade, much larger scale mining operation with increasing annual throughput.
### 2024
#### Commercial production at Enterprise Mine
After two years of commissioning, the largest nickel mine in Africa delivered commercial production ahead of schedule on June 1, 2024.
### 2023
#### Acquisition of La Granja Project
In August 2023, for US$105 million, First Quantum acquired a 55% interest in La Granja, Peru. La Granja is one of the largest undeveloped copper resources. First Quantum will work as operator, in partnership with Rio Tinto.
### 2022
#### Cobre Panamá
Cobre Panamá achieved record copper production of 350kt for the full year, an increase of 6% from 2021.
### 2020
First Quantum Filed NI 43-101 for Taca Taca and Declared Maiden Mineral Reserve of Over 7.7 Million Tonnes of Contained Copper.
#### Ravensthorpe
After significant investment and upgrades, First Quantum’s world-class laterite nickel operation in Western Australia – Ravensthorpe Nickel Operation (“RNO”) – re-started in January 2020 with the first MHP shipment departing Esperance Port just four months later.
### 2019
#### Cobre Panamá
First ore processed at Cobre Panamá.
Cobre Panamá processed its first ore in early February, fifty years after the deposit was discovered, but only six years since First Quantum took it over.
### 2018
#### Cobre Panamá
Construction completed of Cobre Panamá Power Station.
The 300MW power plant generated power at its nameplate capacity and started supplying power into the national grid.
### 2016
#### Sentinel
Commercial production at Sentinel Mine.
For a total capex of US$2.1 billion, and after a year of staged commissioning, Sentinel was declared in commercial production on November 1, 2016.
### 2015
#### Kansanshi smelter
Commissioned Kansanshi smelter.
The Kansanshi smelter achieved commercial production in July, 2015. It was built in two years for a capital cost of US$900m, and brought up to production in an unprecedented 5 months.
### 2014
#### Taca Taca
Acquisition of Taca Taca.
In August 2014, First Quantum acquired Lumina Copper Corporation and its primary asset, the Taca Taca project in Argentina, for approximately CAD$470m. Taca Taca is a copper-gold-molybdenum porphyry deposit in an advanced exploration phase.
### 2013
#### Cobre Panamá
Acquisition of INMET.
Acquired INMET for CAD$5.1 billion, including its 80% stake in the Cobre Panamá project, at the time one of the world’s largest undeveloped copper deposits.
### 2012
#### Kansanshi
First oxide circuit expansion completed at Kansanshi.
Expansion of Kansanshi’s oxide circuit by 20% to 72 mtpa throughput using equipment from the redundant Bwana Mkubwa copper SX-EW plant.
### 2010
#### Trident/Sentinel
Acquisition of Trident/Sentinel.
In January 2010, First Quantum acquired 100% of Kiwara PLC, with a controlling interest in the Trident prospecting license in Zambia which includes the Sentinel copper project and the Enterprise nickel project, for approximately US$260m.
### 2009
#### Kolwezi
Work halted at the Kolwezi tailings project.
First Quantum’s legal title to the Kolwezi project tailings permit was terminated by the DRC Government in September 2009. The project infrastructure was about 75% complete and capex about US$350 million.
### 2008
#### Kansanshi
Expansion project (“S2”) completed at Kansanshi.
Second sulphide milling circuit (“S2”) and fourth SX-EW facility commissioned.
### 2007
#### Frontier
Commercial production at Frontier.
Sean Whittome, General Manager: “Frontier … took just 15 months to build and about five weeks to get to commercial production. And after a further three weeks, we were at full production. So within two months of starting it, it was at full production.”
### 2006
#### Kolwezi
Acquisition of Kolwezi tailings project.
On 1 May 2006, First Quantum acquired Adastra for US$273 million and with it, a 65% interest in the Kolwezi copper-cobalt oxide tailings dams in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
### 2005
#### Kansanshi
Commercial production at Kansanshi.
Commercial production at Kansanshi was achieved in April 2005, just 19 months after construction commenced. Capital additions increased the original nameplate capacity, and by early 2006 Kansanshi was treating 8 mtpa of sulphide ore and 4 mtpa of oxide ore – and had invested a total of approximately US$341m.
### 2004
#### Guelb Moghrein
Acquisition of Guelb Moghrein.
First Quantum acquired the Guelb Moghrein copper-gold project in Mauritania for US$10 million in April 2004.
### 2003
#### Frontier
Greenfields discovery.
First Quantum’s discovery of the Frontier deposit in the Democratic Republic of Congo was the result of generative exploration, found using grid soil sampling in an area of scattered, old surface workings.
### 2001
#### Kansanshi
Acquisition of Kansanshi.
First Quantum acquired Phelps Dodge’s 80% interest in the Kansanshi copper-gold project in Zambia for US$2.5 million cash and 1.4 million shares.
### 2000
#### Lonshi
Greenfields discovery.
The Lonshi copper deposit in the Democratic Republic of Congo was discovered by First Quantum geologists targeting occurences known from archives from the 1920s and 30s.
### 1998
#### Bwana Mkubwa
Completed Bwana Mkubwa processing facility.
Completed construction of the Bwana Mkubwa tailings recovery, SX-EW and sulphuric acid plant. The facility was completed just 16 months after First Quantum acquired the project, for capex of US$32 million.
### 1996
#### Bwana Mkubwa
Acquired the Bwana Mkubwa mining project.
Bwana Mkubwa in Ndola was the first new mining project in Zambia for 35 years and the first licence to be issued by the Government of Zambia since the enactment of the 1995 Mining and Minerals Act.
PreviousNext
> Building the mines we operate gives us a competitive edge
## Expert builders
We build the mines we operate. It provides the opportunity for us to improve our processes at each new project as we live with what we create. Head to our projects page to uncover the operations we are currently building, and find out how we’re growing to meet the copper demands of the future.
[See our operations](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/operations/)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

About
Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make a user's experience more efficient.  
  
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission.  
  
This site uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third party services that appear on our pages.  
  
You can at any time change or withdraw your consent from the Cookie Declaration on our website.  
  
Learn more about who we are, how you can contact us and how we process personal data in our Privacy Policy.  
  
Please state your consent ID and date when you contact us regarding your consent.

# Governance
We recognize our obligation to extract resources responsibly and act with integrity in all our business dealings and operations.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)Governance
## Accountability   
Transparency   
Integrity 
We govern our Company with accountability, transparency and integrity.
The responsibility of ensuring sound corporate governance ultimately lies with the Board of Directors. Our governance responsibility requires us to have regard for employees, investors, local communities, the environment, our suppliers and other stakeholders. Effective governance is an important priority for our Board, as is compliance with the requirements arising from the listing of our securities.
We are listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange ([“TSX:FM”](https://money.tmx.com/en/quote/FM)).
The articles on this page were approved at the Annual and Special Meeting held on May 7, 2020.
## Our Board and Committee members
Our Board of Directors is responsible for the stewardship and long-term success of the business, ensuring we act with integrity towards all parties as we seek to generate value for our shareholders.
[Meet the board](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/FQM-Leadership-Team-Tristan-Pascall.jpg)
## Governance policies
Regularly reviewed, sound governance practices are essential to the wellbeing of a company. Our policies detail our expected behavior in any situation, ensuring clarity in our operational procedures.
[See our policies](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/corporate-governance-documents#policies)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Governance-polices.jpg)
## Governance and articles
Download our key documentation governing First Quantum including our registration documentation, articles of associations and major voting policies.
[See our documents](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/corporate-governance-documents#articles)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Governance-and-articles.jpg)
## Stay up to date 
Sign up to receive updates with insights and stories from around First Quantum, our operations, our projects and our communities.
[Sign up](https://www.first-quantum.com/investors/email-alerts/)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

# Corporate governance  
documents & policies
Our commitment to act responsibly is essential to First Quantum’s success and our ability to generate shareholder value.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)[Governance](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/)Corporate governance documents & policies
Our sound corporate governance practices, and their regular review, are essential to the wellbeing of the Company and its shareholders.
Our governance practices are consistent with the requirements of Canadian National Instrument 58-101 – Disclosure of Corporate Governance Practices and the guidance suggested under National Policy 58-201 – Corporate Governance Guidelines. Our corporate governance framework governs how our Board operates through detailed Board and Board Committee charters which set out clearly how the Board manages its governance obligations. The responsibilities of the Chair are outlined in the [role description](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Chairman-Duties-and-Responsibilities.pdf). On an annual basis, the Board reviews and updates the Board manual which contains details of the governance framework and key policies to ensure that they remain appropriate and relevant.
Our Chair is Kevin McArthur. In this role he leads the Board and is a chief proponent of governance, assisted by the Corporate Secretary.
The Chair of the Nominating and Governance Committee is Kathleen Hogenson, who, together with the Board Chair, oversees the effectiveness of the Board’s individual members, and of its operation as a whole through the annual effectiveness review process. The outcomes and recommendations from the review inform the steps taken to improve Board efficacy, which is monitored throughout the year in consultation with the other Directors.
Further information on these key roles and other key documentation as required by Sec. 473 of the TSX Company Manual can be found below:
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Kevin-McArthur.jpg)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

About
Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make a user's experience more efficient.  
  
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission.  
  
This site uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third party services that appear on our pages.  
  
You can at any time change or withdraw your consent from the Cookie Declaration on our website.  
  
Learn more about who we are, how you can contact us and how we process personal data in our Privacy Policy.  
  
Please state your consent ID and date when you contact us regarding your consent.
**Do not sell or share my personal information**

# Board governance and Committees
Our Board discharges its responsibilities both directly and through four Committees that support its work and decision-making.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)[Governance](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/)Board governance and committees

![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Kevin-McArthur.jpg)
### [Kevin McArthur](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/kevin-mcarthur/)
Board Chair,  
Independent Director
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Kevin-McArthur.jpg)
## Kevin McArthur
### Board Chair,  
Independent Director
Mr McArthur has over 40 years of experience focused on mining operations, corporate development and executive management. He currently serves as a Director of Novagold Resources Inc. Mr McArthur recently served as a Director of Royal Gold, Inc. from 2014 to 2025, a Non-Executive Chair of Boart Longyear Limited from 2019 to 2021, Chief Executive Officer of Tahoe Resources Inc. from 2009 to 2015 and as Executive Chair from 2015 to 2019. Prior experience includes CEO of Goldcorp Inc. from 2006 to 2008 and CEO of Glamis Gold Ltd. from 1999 to 2006. His earlier career focused on mine operations and project development with Glamis Gold, BP Minerals and Homestake Mining Company. Mr McArthur obtained a degree in Mining Engineering from the University of Nevada in 1979.
**Member of: Audit Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/FQM-Leadership-Team-Tristan-Pascall.jpg)
### [Tristan Pascall](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/tristan-pascall/)
Chief Executive Officer
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/FQM-Leadership-Team-Tristan-Pascall.jpg)
[ Follow on Linkedin ](https://www.linkedin.com "Follow on Linkedin")
## Tristan Pascall
### Chief Executive Officer
Tristan Pascall joined First Quantum in 2007. During his time at the Company, he has worked in a variety of site-based roles from pre-development of projects through construction to operational responsibilities. In 2015, he was appointed General Manager of Cobre Panama with responsibility for the operations through the ramp up and commercial production phase. Prior to that, Tristan was part of the group that developed, constructed and operated the Sentinel project in Zambia and also worked on projects at Kansanshi mine and in DRC. Since 2020, Tristan has held executive leadership roles in the Company based in the UK. Before joining First Quantum, Tristan spent eight years in corporate finance and investment banking with a focus on the resources industry. Tristan graduated from the University of Western Australia with a Bachelor of Engineering and Bachelor of Commerce and completed an MBA at INSEAD in France.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Alison-Beckett-1.jpg)
### [Alison Beckett](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/alison-beckett/)
Independent Director,  
Chair Human Resources Committee
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Alison-Beckett-1.jpg)
## Alison Beckett
### Independent Director,  
Chair Human Resources Committee
Independent Director Ms Beckett has a career spanning both industry and consulting, having worked at Conoco (now ConocoPhillips) between 1991 and 2001 in roles across finance, commercial, gas regulations and strategy. From 2001 until 2020 she was an advisor providing leadership advisory services at Egon Zehnder. Currently she is Chair of Governors at Sevenoaks school.
**Member of: Audit Committee and Human Resources Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/FQM-Leadership-Team-Peter-Buzzi.jpg)
### [Peter Buzzi](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/peter-buzzi/)
Non-Independent Director
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/FQM-Leadership-Team-Peter-Buzzi.jpg)
## Peter Buzzi
### Non-Independent Director
Mr Buzzi has more than 35 years of experience in investment banking at RBC Capital Markets (1986 to 2023), including being Co-Head of RBC’s Global Mergers and Acquisitions advisory group and a member of the firm’s investment banking senior leadership team for close to 25 years, retiring as Vice Chair. During this time he advised Canadian and international corporations, private equity firms and institutions on some of the largest and most complex corporate merger, acquisition and restructuring transactions in Canada and abroad, across many industries. He holds a BASc degree in Systems Design Engineering from the University of Waterloo and an MBA from the Harvard Business School. Mr Buzzi is also the Chair of the Board of University of Toronto Schools.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Geoff-Chater-1.jpg)
### [Geoff Chater](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/geoff-chater/)
Independent Director,  
Chair Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Geoff-Chater-1.jpg)
## Geoff Chater
### Independent Director,  
Chair Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee
Mr Chater is a geologist and corporate director with over 35 years of experience in the mineral exploration and mining industries operating worldwide. As a capital markets and corporate strategy consultant, he has focused on transaction-related business development, strategic review, relationship development, defense, mergers/acquisitions, equity finance, and communications. As a director, Mr Chater has been involved in the sale of several public resource companies, including Nevsun Resources, Reservoir Minerals, Valley High Ventures, and Mason Resources. Mr Chater currently serves as a Principal at Namron Advisors. He previously served as an Independent Director at New Gold Inc. (2021-2024), Corporate Relations Manager at First Quantum Minerals Ltd. (1999-2008), President of Valley High Ventures Ltd. (2010-2011), President and CEO at Bearing Resources Ltd. (2011-2012) and Luna Gold Ltd. (2014-2015), Director of Nevsun Resources Ltd. (2016-2018) and Mason Resources Ltd. (2017-2018). Mr Chater is a graduate of Texas Christian University with a Bachelor of Science Degree in Geology.
**Member of: Audit Committee and Nominating & Governance Committee and Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Kathleen-Hogenson.jpg)
### [Kathleen Hogenson ](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/kathleen-hogenson/)
Independent Director,  
Chair Nominating & Governance Committee
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Kathleen-Hogenson.jpg)
## Kathleen Hogenson 
### Independent Director,  
Chair Nominating & Governance Committee
Ms. Hogenson has extensive operational, leadership and executive experience in the oil and gas sector worldwide having served as an executive at Santos Limited and Unocal Corporation. Currently, she is the Chief Executive Officer of Zone Oil & Gas, a company she founded in 2008. Ms. Hogenson is also an independent director at Verisk Analytics, a New Jersey based publicly traded data analytics and risk assessment firm and a director at Tamarack Valley Energy Ltd., a Calgary based publicly traded oil & gas upstream operator. She previously served on the board of Parallel Petroleum LLC, Cimarex Energy Co. and in an advisory role at Samsung Oil & Gas, LLC and Samsung C&T from 2008 to 2015. She also serves on the Advisory Board of The Women’s Global Leadership Conference and was a speaker at the Harvard Business School Women’s Conference. Ms. Hogenson earned a Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering from The Ohio State University.
**Member of: Nominating & Governance Committee and Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/FQM-Leadership-Team-Juanita-Montalvo.jpg)
### [Juanita Montalvo](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/juanita-montalvo/)
Independent Director
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/FQM-Leadership-Team-Juanita-Montalvo.jpg)
## Juanita Montalvo
### Independent Director
Ms. Montalvo has over 25 years of governance, executive, operations and investment experience in the mining, extractive and agricultural industries in various jurisdictions, including Latin America and Africa. She is a Managing Partner at Privus Capital Inc., focused on private equity and strategic corporate investments, a Non-Executive Director of Moranda Metals and the Board Chair of Dundee Precious Metals. Ms. Montalvo has held various leadership roles, including as Senior Vice President Corporate Affairs and Sustainability at Sherritt International Corporation and Country Manager in Madagascar during the construction of the Ambatovy Joint Venture. She is the Chairman of the Wildlife Conservation Society Canada and a founding member of the Women for Nature initiative of Nature Canada. She holds a B.Sc. in Biology and Biochemistry, a B.A. in International Development Studies, and a Masters in Development Economics, all from Dalhousie University. She is also part of McKinsey’s LGBTQ Leadership Master Class Alumni and has the ICD.D designation from the Institute of Corporate Directors and Rotman School of Management.
**Member of: Human Resources Committee and Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/FQM-Leadership-Team-Brian-Nichols_rs-1024x1024.jpg)
### [Brian Nichols](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/brian-nichols/)
Independent Director
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/FQM-Leadership-Team-Brian-Nichols_rs.jpg)
## Brian Nichols
### Independent Director
Brian A. Nichols has over 35 years of experience in international relations, crisis management, and trade development across Latin America, Africa, and Asia. Most recently, he served as Assistant Secretary of State for Western Hemisphere Affairs, where he directed U.S. policy across the region. He managed large foreign assistance and operational budgets, driving initiatives that strengthened economic partnerships, supported trade and investment, and promoted sustainable development.
Previously, he served as U.S. Ambassador to Zimbabwe and Peru and in multiple other senior diplomatic roles. Throughout his career, Amb. Nichols has championed economic growth, built strategic public-private partnerships, and advanced legal and regulatory frameworks to support international investment.
Amb. Nichols is a recognized global policy and trade leader, having received numerous awards for his contributions to American foreign policy. He holds a B.A. in Political Science from Tufts University.
**Member of: Nominating & Governance Committee and Human Resources Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Simon-Scott.jpg)
### [Simon Scott](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/simon-scott/)
Independent Director,  
Chair Audit Committee
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/FQM-Leadership-Team-Simon-Scott.jpg)
## Simon Scott
### Independent Director,  
Chair Audit Committee
Mr Scott has over 20 years of experience in the mining industry. Between 2010 and 2016, he was Chief Financial Officer of Lonmin plc, a platinum mining company listed on the London Stock Exchange and was acting CEO between 2012 and 2013. Prior to that, Mr Scott was Chief Financial Officer of Aveng Limited, a Johannesburg Stock Exchange listed construction company providing products and services to the mining industry globally. Mr Scott also held various senior management positions in Anglo American Platinum Limited, including acting CFO. His early career was spent in various financial positions, including CFO of Southern Africa for JP Morgan Chase. Mr Scott is a Chartered Accountant and holds degrees in both accounting and commerce from the University of the Witwatersrand in South Africa. He previously served on the board of AngloGold Ashanti Holdings plc., a global gold mining company (2019 – 2024). He is currently a Non-Executive Director of Sylvania Platinum Limited, a PGMs producing company listed on the London Stock Exchange’s Alternative Investment Market. As of 1 July 2024, Mr Scott was appointed Independent Non-Executive Director of Gemfields Group Limited.
**Member of: Human Resources Committee**
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/FQM-Leadership-Team-Hanjun-Xia-1.jpg)
### [Kevin Xia](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/leadership/hanjun-xia/)
Independent Director
Read More
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/FQM-Leadership-Team-Hanjun-Xia-1.jpg)
## Kevin Xia
### Independent Director
Mr. Xia has over 20 years of experience in the global copper industry, covering the entire industrial chain from mining, smelting and processing to marketing and trading. Mr. Xia is currently at Jiangxi Copper Company Limited, holding various roles since 2001, including Coordinator in the Department of Overseas Economic and Technical Cooperation, International Cooperation Project Manager, Investor and Government Relations Manager, Director of the Office for Chairman and CEO and, more recently, President of Marketing and Trading.
**Member of: Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee**
## Roles and responsibilities
Each of our Independent Directors typically serve on two Board committees. All of the Committees and their work are governed by Board approved charters.
**Audit Committee**  
The Audit Committee’s primary function is to assist the Board with oversight of financial reporting and control responsibilities.
**Human Resources Committee**  
The Human Resources Committee assists the Board with its decision making regarding First Quantum’s approach to compensation plans, including those for named executive officers, and reviews and recommends the CEO’s compensation for approval by the Board.
**Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee**  
The Safety, Environmental & Social Performance Committee assists the Board with respect to monitoring First Quantum’s environmental, health, safety and CSR policies and practices in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
**Nominating and Governance Committee**  
The Nominating & Governance Committee has oversight of the composition, effectiveness and compensation of the Board, as well as its corporate governance practices.

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

# Leadership
Read more about the people leading our business into the future.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)Leadership
## Strong leadership and governance drive long-term success
Effective leadership, supported by robust governance, is crucial for the long-term success of First Quantum. Our management team leads the day-to-day business and operations, acting with integrity towards employees, investors, and host communities. This approach is essential for generating shareholder value and ensuring the company’s sustained performance.
## Board of Directors
Our Board of Directors provides independent oversight and governance, holding management accountable for the company’s performance. The Board’s primary role is to ensure First Quantum is well-managed and operating in the best interests of its shareholders and other key stakeholders, including employees and local communities. The Board also considers the impacts of our operations on the environment and society.
**The key roles of the Board include:**
  * **Strategic Oversight:** The board reviews and approves the company’s overall strategy proposed by management, ensuring it aligns with long-term goals and risk tolerance.
  * **Risk Governance:** The board oversees the risk management framework to ensure that management identifies, assesses, and mitigates significant risks.
  * **Leadership and Succession:** The board is responsible for appointing, compensating, and overseeing the senior management team, including the CEO, and planning for their succession.
  * **Financial and Reporting Integrity:** The board ensures the integrity of financial reporting and internal controls.
  * **Ethical and Environmental Stewardship:** The board oversees how the company manages its responsibilities regarding human health, safety, and environmental protection.


The Board’s work is supported by four [Committees of the Board](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/governance/board-governance-and-committees/) which operate under specific Charters and enable detailed review of a range of policy and regulatory areas before decisions are made by Directors.
The Board of Directors and Management Group are also supported by key financial, operational and compliance staff.

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

About
Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make a user's experience more efficient.  
  
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission.  
  
This site uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third party services that appear on our pages.  
  
You can at any time change or withdraw your consent from the Cookie Declaration on our website.  
  
Learn more about who we are, how you can contact us and how we process personal data in our Privacy Policy.

![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Our-culture_-hero_130126.jpg)
# Our culture
When talented people are given the freedom to think independently, challenge convention and act on their best ideas: extraordinary things can be achieved.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)Our culture
## Decision makers at every level
Our culture emphasizes self-reliance and simple hard work.
It is nimble, enabling us to make decisions quickly, encouraging innovation as we build and operate our assets to maximize their potential, for ourselves, society and the communities in which we work.
## Bolder, smarter,   
driven, together.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Bolder.jpg)
## Bolder
Challenges are faced head on with adaptive and forward thinking. We build and operate our projects in countries others deemed too hard to enter and in environments they felt were too inaccessible. We forge our own path instead of following the beaten track.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Smarter.jpg)
## Smarter
Innovation is deeply embedded into our Company’s foundational framework. Our curiosity fosters a culture of continuous learning, challenges conventions and drives innovation as a means of enhancing our performance, achieving operational excellence and delivering long-term value. It transforms our business as we help create a greener economy for the future.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/FQM_Driven_V1_res.jpg)
## Driven
We’re focused on achieving the goals we set for ourselves. It’s integral to who we are, as people who get things done with a clear focus on reaching the end goal and finding the best way to get there. We are motivated to enhance our performance, fostering a culture of continuous learning and challenging the status quo.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Together.jpg)
## Together
We are stronger together and with a shared vision to achieve our full potential. Whether it’s through our collaboration to innovate and find better ways of working, or in our interactions with society and local communities in the places that we work, we know that we work best when we all take part, together.
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4
PreviousNext
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Rudi-Badenhorst_outside_comp.jpg)
> “A strong focus on resilience and adaptability is in our DNA. We take on challenges with an adaptive mindset – it empowers us to reinvent mining as a force of good in the world.”
**Rudi Badenhorst,**  
Chief Operating Officer
![Anthony Mukutuma](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Anthonys_23_years_at_FQM.jpg)
> “At our core is an entrepreneurial spirit. Individuals are encouraged to re-imagine, challenge & then implement change.”
**Anthony Mukutuma,**  
Director, Zambia
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Portrait-quote-1024x768-Our-culture.jpg)
> “First Quantum’s projects are exciting and fast paced, this energy defines us and drives us all to achieve more. We deliver on ambitious goals.”
**Sean Egner,**  
Project Manager
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Portrait-quote-1024x768-Our-culture-2.jpg)
> “Success is seldom achieved in isolation, and we’re no exception. Together, we seek meaningful ways to work, to improve lives and to build a brighter future.”
**Steven Lewis,**  
Manager, Project Development
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4
PreviousNext
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Responsible_mining.jpg)
## Responsible   
mining
We are expert responsible miners with a unique approach and a proven track record. We focus on extracting materials that are vital to society. We know we can only succeed when we respect and look after the people and places where we operate.
[Find out about our operations](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/operations/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Find-a-carrer-banner-image-1.jpg)
## Find a career,   
not a job
We hire and train experts, helping every colleague to achieve their full potential. Our lean culture provides the freedom to help us grow and shape our own careers. If you’re looking to start your career, we have fantastic learning opportunities. Careers at First Quantum are as diverse as the locations we work in around the world.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/careers/)
## Stay up to date 
Sign up to receive updates with insights and stories from around First Quantum, our operations, our projects and our communities.
[Sign up](https://www.first-quantum.com/investors/email-alerts/)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/What-we-do-Hero.jpg)![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/FQM_Hero_What_We_Do_Front.webp)
# What we do
First Quantum is a global copper company. We operate long life mines in five continents, employing approximately 14,500 people.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)What we do
## We extract materials that are vital to society
For 25 years, we’ve been forging our own path, employing local people and empowering leaders at every level to make decisions to maximize the value we create and share that with the people and places where we work.
We are known for our lean culture which has helped us gain a track record of success in tough conditions. Combined with our specialist technical, engineering, construction and operational skills, it means we expertly develop and responsibly operate world-class copper mines.
396kt
copper produced in 2025
US$2.4BN
spent in local procurement from nationally registered suppilers
+95%
of employees are nationals in the countries in which we operate
## Efficient   
operations
Our operations span nine countries in five continents. We know our mines are at their best when they’re supported by the local community. It’s why we employ locally and take care of the places in which we operate.
In addition to copper, we also mine nickel and provide a variety of additional metals, extracted as part of our copper and nickel operations.
[See our operations](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/operations/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1054.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1053.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-955-1.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1056.jpg)
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4
PreviousNext
## Meeting future   
demands
We’re building for the future with our development projects, focused mainly in South America. These projects will help us to increase our capacity as we seek to help meet the ever-growing demand for copper.
We design and deliver our own projects. It allows us to continuously learn from our work as we build ever more efficient operations to meet a societal need.
[See our projects](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/development-projects/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1069-1.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1063.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1066.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1065.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1067-1.jpg)
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4View slide 5
PreviousNext
## Essential   
resources
The resources we mine are essential for society. Whether it is through supporting the green energy transition as a key component of car batteries, wind turbines and power grids, or in the everyday items that many of us take for granted, such as phones, computers or heating, copper is ubiquitous in modern society.
[Find out more about our commodities](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/our-commodities/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1051.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1052.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1048.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Rectangle-1281.jpg)
View slide 1View slide 2View slide 3View slide 4
PreviousNext
## Responsibly   
sourced
We care about the people and the places where we work and seek to limit any negative impacts from our business whilst we help deliver a key building block of society.
We know that we need the resources we mine in order to build the society that we want, but we also respect that we can’t do that whilst compromising the earth we live on or the lives and livelihoods of the people where we work.
[Find out about our sustainability work](https://www.first-quantum.com/sustainability/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1072.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1044.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1071.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Rectangle-1070.jpg)

## Become   
a trusted   
supplier
**Local suppliers**  
We actively search for and work with local suppliers at each of our operations and projects. We want to hear from experts who share our values and are able to help us achieve our goals. Your journey to become a trusted supplier could start today.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/suppliers/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Find-a-carrer-banner-image-1.jpg)
## Find a career,   
not a job
We hire and train experts, helping every colleague to achieve their full potential. Our lean culture provides the freedom to help us grow and shape our own careers. If you’re looking to start your career, we have fantastic learning opportunities. Careers at First Quantum are as diverse as the locations we work in around the world.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/careers/)

Sign up to receive updates with insights and stories from around First Quantum, our operations, our projects and our communities.

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. ![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Our-Purpose-Hero.jpg)
# Our purpose and values
As expert responsible miners, we work to meet society’s needs without it costing the Earth.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[About us](https://www.first-quantum.com/about-us/)Our purpose and values
## Our purpose
We work responsibly to provide metals that build the modern world.
We recognize the need for mining to responsibly deliver the critical metals essential for the world, but we can’t do that unless we have a positive impact locally and on the broader global environment.
First Quantum believes in the value that mining can bring, not only for our people, investors and host governments but also for the communities we are part of and for the environment in which we operate. Our approach to responsible mining is guided by core values developed in the Company’s formative years and the practical requirement to deliver results on the ground. Being honest with ourselves and remaining true to our values is key to achieving the best results. We take the right course of action because it is the right thing to do, and encourage each of our employees to take responsibility for their own actions.
## As responsible miners, we are
## Environmentally sensitive
We recognize that we share natural resources such as clean air, water and energy. As such, we have a core commitment to seek and manage these resources responsibly. That means endeavouring to optimize energy consumption through a continued prioritization of innovation, improving efficiencies and reducing wastage.  
We recognize our responsibility towards biodiversity both within our direct areas of influence and the broader environment and continually explore opportunities to ‘give back’ to the habitat impacted by our operations.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Environmentaly-sensitive.jpg)
## Socially responsible
Our relationships are based on transparency, trust and respect, recognizing that our activities affect or could affect our stakeholders, local communities, their cultures, traditional and current uses of land and resources. The welfare of our people and the communities in which we operate is important for building lasting relationships. We seek to ensure that the positive economic impacts of mining are realized and to assist in improving the quality of life for those people and communities impacted by our activities.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Socially-responsible.jpg)
## Committed to colleagues
Safe production is our top priority. We believe that there is no job that cannot be done safely. That means empowering and supporting our colleagues through practical systems and training, including our THINK! campaign.
Our people are vitally important to our ongoing success and to our practical, outcomes-driven and innovative culture. Developing our people, empowering and mentoring those around us, has made us what we are today. We want our personnel to grow with us and help drive our future growth.
The strength in our differences enables us to recognize the distinct and different needs of those around us. This means embracing diversity in our workforce, empowering people to speak up with new ideas and innovation, as well as to address areas of concern and for improvement.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Commiteed-to-colleagues.jpg)
## Governed openly and robustly
Our culture expects responsibility and accountability in how we operate. Our dynamic organization structure means that our people are empowered to challenge and continually strive to do better. Established governance structures are in place to support this, from site management through to executive and Board oversight. We have developed a wide range of risk management methodologies and deploy resources to understand, manage and mitigate risk throughout the Company’s operations. This is subject to regular and ongoing oversight and monitoring by our executive management and the First Quantum Board.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Governed-openly-and-robustly.jpg)
## Sustainable, driven by innovation
Innovation is key to our approach to sustainability. Continued innovation has an important role to play as we seek to deliver metals in the most sustainable and cost-effective manner. As a responsible miner, this means improving how we use energy and other resources within our business. Appropriate energy sources are sourced, and technology leveraged, to reduce the resources that we require. We are focused on delivering tangible projects from mine to mill that will deliver measurable changes in performance.
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sustainable-driven-by-innovation.jpg)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Find-a-carrer-banner-image-1.jpg)
## Find a career,   
not a job
We hire and train experts, helping every colleague to achieve their full potential. Our lean culture provides the freedom to help us grow and shape our own careers. If you’re looking to start your career, we have fantastic learning opportunities. Careers at First Quantum are as diverse as the locations we work in around the world.
[Find out more](https://www.first-quantum.com/careers/)
![](https://www.first-quantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Responsible_mining.jpg)
## Responsible   
mining
We are expert responsible miners with a unique approach and a proven track record. We focus on extracting materials that are vital to society. We know we can only succeed when we respect and look after the people and places where we operate.
[Find out about our operations](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/operations/)
## Stay up to date 
Sign up to receive updates with insights and stories from around First Quantum, our operations, our projects and our communities.
[Sign up](https://www.first-quantum.com/investors/email-alerts/)

## Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures and Ratios
Our financial results are prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This Website and the External Site refers to a number of non-GAAP measures and ratios which are not measures recognized under IFRS and do not have a standardized meaning prescribed by IFRS or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) in the United States. Information disclosed in the External Site may include non-GAAP measures, which do not have standardized meanings prescribed under IFRS, may differ from those used by other issuers, and and are not necessarily comparable to similar measures presented by other issuers. These measures and ratios have been derived from our financial statements and applied on a consistent basis as appropriate; are used internally by management in measuring the performance of the Company’s operations and serve to provide additional information which should not be considered in isolation to measures and ratios prepared under GAAP. First Quantum discloses these measures in order to provide assistance in understanding the results of the operations and to provide additional information to investors. These measures and ratios should not be considered in isolation or viewed as a substitute for, or or superior to, other measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. For further description of the non-GAAP measures and ratios that may appear, including reconciliations, are provided in the MD&A accompanying the Company’s financial statements filed from time to time on SEDAR+ at [www.sedarplus.com](https://www.sedarplus.com).

# Development projects
Our development projects are the future of our business, helping us answer the exponential demand for copper.
[Home](https://www.first-quantum.com)[What we do](https://www.first-quantum.com/what-we-do/)Development projects
## Our pathway to one million tonnes of annual production 
Our development projects are expected to push us to more than one million tonnes of annual copper production, making us one of the world’s top copper producers. These new projects are vital if we, as an industry, are to meet the expected future demand for copper.
## Where we operate 
Our operations and development projects span five continents and diverse conditions. Visit each location to find out more about our mineral producing assets and new prospects.

## Lifecycle of a Mine
We develop and operate our mines, leveraging our internal expertise to ensure continuous improvement throughout each project’s lifecycle.
Stage 1
### Exploration 
After identifying a mineral deposit, we map, sample, and assess its size, grade, and economic potential. We engage local stakeholders, plan responsibly, and secure permits while developing environmental and social management plans. This phase lays the blueprint for sustainable development.
Stage 2
### Development
We build the infrastructure needed for mining including roads, processing plants, utilities, and waste facilities. Equipment and technology are installed, and all systems are designed to meet high safety and environmental standards, readying the site for long-term production.
Stage 3
### Operation 
Full-scale mining begins. We extract, transport, crush, grind, and process ore through flotation and dewatering. These processes – the systems for which we are experts in constructing – produce a concentrate from the ore. In some cases, the concentrate is shipped for smelting while in others we smelt onsite. This stage spans decades and reflects our operational expertise.
Stage 4
### Closure
As production winds down, we restore the site through land regrading, revegetation, and infrastructure decommissioning. Working with stakeholders, we ensure our environmental and community responsibilities are fulfilled, leaving behind a responsible legacy.
Stage 5
### Aftercare
We continue monitoring the site post-closure – tracking water, soil, and ecological health. Issues are addressed promptly to ensure long-term environmental stability, reflecting our commitment to stewardship beyond the mine’s lifespan.

## We look after the people and the places where we operate
Environmental stewardship and community engagement are essential for the success of our projects and operations. We can only succeed when we look after the people and the places where we operate. For us, this means creating programs in partnership with the local community. It means creating jobs and providing training to help people take those roles on. And it means working alongside local governments and leaders to find ways to ensure we’re looking after the environment too.
